## TIER 2 BLOCK
*(Included for implementation tasks. < 2,000 tokens total with Tier 1.)*

### Architecture in 5 Sentences

Hyperspace is a Go-native publish/subscribe messaging platform that delivers sub-100-microsecond P99 same-AZ latency at one million messages per second by separating the client hot path (shared-memory log buffers, zero system calls) from the network transport (an out-of-process driver daemon, `hsd`). The client library (`pkg/client`) writes messages to mmap'd log buffers via `Publication.Offer()` and reads them via `Subscription.Poll()` without any network interaction; the `hsd` daemon runs five cooperating goroutine-based agents (Conductor, Sender, Receiver, PathManager, PoolManager) that move data between log buffers and a pool of N concurrent QUIC connections per peer. Each QUIC connection runs an independent congestion control loop (CUBIC, BBRv1, BBRv3, or a DRL controller via ONNX Runtime), exploiting AWS EC2's ECMP path diversity to sustain 50 Gbps link utilisation. Peer discovery uses AWS Cloud Map, runtime configuration uses SSM Parameter Store, TLS certificates are fetched from Secrets Manager, and all connections are mutually authenticated using short-lived X.509 SVIDs from SPIFFE/SPIRE with automatic rotation. The system targets AWS EC2 c7gn.4xlarge (arm64, 50 Gbps) and requires no persistent message storage — it is a live transport layer, not a broker.

### Component Registry

| Component | Path | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Log Buffer | `pkg/logbuffer/` | Stable | Three-term mmap rotation; ≥ 90% coverage |
| Ring Buffers / IPC | `pkg/ipc/ringbuffer/`, `pkg/ipc/broadcast/`, `pkg/ipc/memmap/` | Stable | SPSC, MPSC, broadcast; ≥ 90% coverage |
| QUIC Transport Adapter | `pkg/transport/quic/` | Stable | quic-go wrapper; TLS 1.3 minimum |
| Connection Pool | `pkg/transport/pool/` | Stable | N concurrent connections per peer |
| Arbitrator | `pkg/transport/arbitrator/` | Stable | LowestRTT, Hybrid, Sticky, Random, LeastOutstanding |
| Probe Frames | `pkg/transport/probes/` | Stable | PING/PONG wire format |
| Conductor | `pkg/driver/conductor/` | Stable | Control plane agent |
| Sender | `pkg/driver/sender/` | Stable | Outbound data plane agent |
| Receiver | `pkg/driver/receiver/` | Stable | Inbound data plane agent |
| PathManager | `pkg/driver/pathmgr/` | Stable | EWMA RTT probing; 95.1% coverage |
| PoolManager + AdaptivePoolLearner | `pkg/driver/poolmgr/` | Stable | Lifecycle + learner; 96.5% / 86.2% |
| Client Library | `pkg/client/` | Stable | Client, Publication, Subscription, Image |
| Channel URI Parser | `pkg/channel/` | Stable | `hs:quic?endpoint=...` |
| Congestion Control | `pkg/cc/cubic/`, `pkg/cc/bbr/`, `pkg/cc/bbrv3/`, `pkg/cc/drl/` | Stable | DRL gated by `//go:build onnx` |
| Counters | `pkg/counters/` | Stable | Named atomic int64 in cnc.dat; 95.2% |
| Observability | `pkg/obs/` | Stable | OTel traces/metrics; CloudWatch export |
| AWS Discovery | `pkg/discovery/` | Stable | Cloud Map resolver; 82.7% |
| AWS Config | `pkg/config/` | Stable | SSM Parameter Store; 100% |
| Identity | `pkg/identity/` | Stable | SPIFFE SVID + Secrets Manager cert |
| AtomicBuffer | `internal/atomic/` | Stable | Little-endian int64 ops over mmap |
| hsd daemon | `cmd/hsd/` | Stable | 4.7 MiB binary; arm64 |
| hyperspace-stat CLI | `cmd/hyperspace-stat/` | Stable | Real-time counter table |
| hyperspace-probe | `cmd/hyperspace-probe/` | Stable | Benchmark tool |
| Examples | `examples/` | Stable | basic_publisher, basic_subscriber, a2a_echo |

### Key Interfaces

```go
// CongestionController — pkg/cc
type CongestionController interface {
    OnAck(ackedBytes int64, rtt time.Duration)
    OnLoss(lostBytes int64)
    CongestionWindow() int64
}

// Connection — pkg/transport/quic
type Connection interface {
    Send(frames []Frame) error
    Recv() (Frame, error)
    Close() error
    RTT() time.Duration
    RemoteAddr() net.Addr
}

// Agent lifecycle — all five driver agents
type Agent interface {
    Run(ctx context.Context) error
}

// Provider pattern — pkg/identity
type SVIDProvider interface {
    FetchSVID(ctx context.Context) (*tls.Certificate, *x509.CertPool, error)
    Watch(ctx context.Context, onChange func(*tls.Certificate, *x509.CertPool))
}
```

### Channel URI Format

```
hs:quic?endpoint=10.0.0.1:7070|pool=4|cc=bbrv3
```

Parameters separated by `|`. Defaults: `pool=4`, `cc=bbrv3`. The `endpoint` parameter is required.

### Active ADRs

- ADR-001: Multi-QUIC pool (N=4 connections per peer, ECMP diversity) — Accepted 2026-04-17
- ADR-002: QUIC via quic-go as sole transport (not Aeron reliable UDP) — Accepted 2026-04-17
- ADR-003: DRL congestion control via ONNX Runtime + CGO (restricted to `pkg/cc/drl`) — Accepted 2026-04-17
- ADR-004: SPIFFE/SPIRE for workload identity, full integration — Accepted 2026-04-17
- ADR-005: Embedded driver mode for integration testing (no subprocess) — Accepted 2026-04-17
- ADR-006: Real AWS integrations from day one via Localstack — Accepted 2026-04-17

## TIER 3 BLOCK
*(Included for planning, architecture, and evaluation tasks. < 5,000 tokens total.)*

### System Architecture Reference

Full architecture: `SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md`. Key sections:
- Section 1: Overview — Aeron DNA, QUIC transport, AWS EC2 c7gn.4xlarge target
- Section 2: System Context (C4 L1) — Publisher App, Subscriber App, Hyperspace, AWS services, SPIRE
- Section 3: Container Diagram (C4 L2) — pkg/client, hsd daemon, QUIC pool, cnc.dat
- Section 4: Component Diagram (C4 L3) — Five driver agents, arbitrator, pool
- Section 5: Data Flows — Publish path, subscribe path, cert rotation path
- Section 6: Non-functional requirements — P99 < 100 µs, 1.5 M msg/s, TLS 1.3
- Section 7: Security architecture — mTLS, SPIFFE trust domain, mmap 0600, gosec

### Full ADR Log

See `decision_log.md` — 6 ADRs, all Accepted, all dated 2026-04-17.

### Performance Targets

| Metric | Target | Measurement |
|---|---|---|
| Same-AZ P99 RTT at 1 M msg/s | < 100 µs | EC2 benchmark harness (`cmd/hyperspace-probe`) |
| IPC RTT (same-host, embedded) | < 1 µs | `BenchmarkAppender` in `pkg/logbuffer` |
| Peak sustained throughput | ≥ 1.5 M msg/s | EC2 benchmark harness |
| Driver restart recovery | < 500 ms | Integration test |
| Counter write latency | < 1 µs | `BenchmarkCounter` in `pkg/counters` |

### AWS Integration Details

| AWS Service | Package | Auth | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Cloud Map | `pkg/discovery` | IAM role | `DiscoverInstances` API; `host:port` output |
| SSM Parameter Store | `pkg/config` | IAM role | `GetParameter`; `ParameterNotFound` → default |
| Secrets Manager | `pkg/identity` | IAM role | PEM cert+key bundle by ARN |
| CloudWatch | `pkg/obs` | IAM role | `Hyperspace/` namespace; 60 s flush interval |

**Localstack:** `AWS_ENDPOINT_URL=http://localhost:4566` overrides all AWS API endpoints. Docker Compose starts Localstack in CI. Integration tests in `pkg/discovery`, `pkg/config`, `pkg/identity` use the real AWS SDK against Localstack.

### SPIFFE/SPIRE Configuration

| Parameter | Default | Override |
|---|---|---|
| SPIRE Agent socket | `unix:///run/spire/agent.sock` | SSM `hyperspace/spire/socket_path` |
| SVID TTL | 1 hour (SPIRE Server config) | Operator-configured |
| Trust domain | `cloud-jumpgate.example.com` | Operator-configured |
| Rotation watch | Before-expiry (go-spiffe Watch API) | Automatic |
